skill.name: gate-exchange-futures
skill.description: The USDT perpetual futures trading function of Gate Exchange: open position, close position, cancel order, amend order. Trigger phrases: open position, close position, cancel order, amend order, reverse, close all.
skill.summary: Trading skill for Gate Exchange USDT perpetual futures with four core operations: open, close, cancel, and amend positions.
skill.author: gate

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 373 potential issues, all are false positives. The skill contains only markdown documentation files with code examples using backticks for formatting. No executable code exists. The skill provides safe trading workflows for Gate Exchange futures using MCP tools, requires user confirmation for all trading operations, and handles no credentials.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Blocks Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 373 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across all markdown files. These are false positives - backticks are used for markdown code formatting in documentation, not for executing commands. The skill contains only .md documentation files with no executable code.

content.user_title: Trade Gate Exchange Futures with AI
content.value_statement: Execute USDT perpetual futures trades on Gate Exchange through natural language. Open, close, cancel, and amend positions with confirmation safeguards.
actual_capabilities: Open long or short positions with limit or market orders on USDT perpetual futures
Close positions fully or partially, or reverse positions entirely
Cancel single orders or batch cancel multiple orders by contract
Amend existing order prices or sizes with precision validation
Handle cross and isolated margin modes with user confirmation
Support both single and dual position modes with appropriate API selection
limitations: Requires Gate MCP server to be configured and connected before use
Only supports USDT perpetual futures contracts on Gate Exchange
Cannot monitor markets or execute arbitrage strategies automatically
User must provide all required parameters such as contract, size, and price

use_cases.title: Quick Position Entry
use_cases.description: Traders can rapidly open long or short positions using natural language commands like 'Open long 1 contract BTC_USDT at 65000' without navigating complex trading interfaces.

use_cases.title: Risk Management Automation
use_cases.description: Users can close partial positions to lock in profits, reverse positions entirely, or cancel all pending orders for a contract with single commands like 'Close half' or 'Cancel all orders'.

use_cases.title: Order Modification Workflow
use_cases.description: Traders can amend existing order prices or sizes through conversation using commands like 'Change that buy order price to 64000' after market conditions change.

prompt_templates.title: Open Basic Position
prompt_templates.prompt: Open long 1 contract BTC_USDT at market price
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner opening their first futures position with market execution

prompt_templates.title: Open Position with Limits
prompt_templates.prompt: BTC_USDT long 100U, limit 65000, 10x leverage, isolated margin
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced trader specifying all parameters for precise position control

prompt_templates.title: Close and Reverse
prompt_templates.prompt: Reverse my BTC_USDT position to short
prompt_templates.scenario: Trader changing market outlook and wanting to flip position direction

prompt_templates.title: Batch Order Management
prompt_templates.prompt: Cancel all open orders for ETH_USDT and show my current position
prompt_templates.scenario: Trader cleaning up orders before reassessing market strategy

output_examples.input: Open long 1 contract BTC_USDT at 65000 with 5x leverage
output_examples.output: Order Summary: BTC_USDT LONG, 1 contract, Limit 65000, Cross margin, 5x leverage. Estimated margin: 13000 USDT. Reply 'confirm' to place the order.
Found 2 BTC_USDT positions: LONG 1.5 contracts, SHORT 0.5 contracts. Close all positions? Reply to confirm.
Amended order #12345678: Price updated from 65000 to 64000 USDT. Size unchanged at 1 contract.

best_practices: Always review the order summary before confirming trades, especially the leverage and estimated margin requirements
Specify contract names precisely (e.g., BTC_USDT, ETH_USDT) to avoid errors when multiple contracts exist
Use limit orders for better price control instead of market orders during volatile market conditions
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill without first configuring and connecting the Gate MCP server
Avoid opening positions without confirming the leverage setting, as higher leverage increases liquidation risk
Do not attempt to switch margin modes when holding opposing positions in dual mode without closing positions first

faq.question: What futures contracts does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports all USDT perpetual futures contracts available on Gate Exchange, including BTC_USDT, ETH_USDT, and other major cryptocurrency pairs.

faq.question: Do I need to provide my API keys to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill does not handle credentials directly. You must configure the Gate MCP server separately with your API keys before using this skill.

faq.question: Can I automate trades without confirmation?
faq.answer: No, all trading operations require your explicit confirmation before execution. This safety feature prevents accidental trades.

faq.question: What is the difference between cross and isolated margin modes?
faq.answer: Cross margin shares your balance across all positions, while isolated margin restricts margin to a single position. This skill supports both modes.

faq.question: How do I reverse a position?
faq.answer: Simply say 'Reverse my [CONTRACT] position' or 'Reverse to short/long'. The skill will close your current position and open a new one in the opposite direction.

faq.question: Can I amend multiple orders at once?
faq.answer: No, this skill only supports amending one order at a time. Specify the order by ID or describe which order you want to modify.
